hero.desc: Advancing knowledge at the intersection of environmental exposures and human health through rigorous analytical science, biomarker research, and interdisciplinary collaboration.

pi.name: Muhammad Saiful Islam Khan
pi.bio: Dr. Muhammad Saiful Islam Khan is an Associate Professor at the School of Medicine, Central Asian University. His research focuses on non-thermal plasma sterilisation, food safety, single particle analysis, pesticide degradation, and advanced analytical chemistry including HPLC and LC-MS.

research.title: Non-thermal Plasma & Food Safety
research.what: Microbial inactivation of pathogens in both planktonic state and biofilm using atmospheric plasma; plasma decontamination of pesticides from fresh produce; plasma inactivation mechanism via individual radicals generated by plasma on microbes.
research.methods: Atmospheric plasma (DBD), submerged plasma reactors, biofilm assays, SEM/EDX, ATR-FTIR, HPLC, LC-MS.
research.why: Non-thermal plasma is a powerful chemical-free tool for food decontamination, extending shelf life while preserving nutritional quality and avoiding harmful residues.

research.title: Single Particle Analysis (SPA) & Aerosol Science
research.what: Establishment of Single Particle Analytical (SPA) technique using SEM/EDX and ATR-FTIR in combination for rapid microbial detection and environmental aerosol characterisation, including asbestos mineral analysis.
research.methods: Low-Z particle EPMA, ATR-FTIR imaging, ED-EPMA, quantitative single-particle analysis.
research.why: Asbestos (Mg-silicate minerals) is a cause of mesothelioma and a potential health risk in developing countries; accurate characterisation is essential for environmental health protection.

research.title: Environmental Contaminant & Pesticide Degradation
research.what: Study of pesticide removal and degradation pathways in water using microplasma; identification of byproducts by LC-MS; mechanistic and kinetic studies of contaminant breakdown.
research.methods: Microplasma discharge, LC-MS, ozonation, role-of-radical analysis, kinetic modelling.
research.why: Pesticide contamination of water threatens ecosystems and human health; understanding degradation mechanisms enables more effective water treatment strategies.

research.title: Food Chemistry & Quality Analysis
research.what: Evaluation of chemical quality of plasma-treated food — including flavonoid and ascorbic acid extraction — and physical quality changes of treated food samples.
research.methods: HPLC, LC-MS, UV-Vis spectrophotometry, extraction workflows, quality assays.
research.why: Links environmental exposures and food processing interventions to clinical and nutritional outcomes, supporting evidence-based food safety policy.

research.title: Analytical Method Development
research.what: Build reliable, reproducible analytical protocols for complex environmental, biological, and food matrices. Method validation across a range of compound classes.
research.methods: Full validation (precision, linearity, LOD/LOQ), certified reference materials, QC samples, SPE clean-up.
research.why: Robust methods make results trustworthy, publishable, and reproducible — underpinning all downstream research conclusions.

news.title: New Paper Accepted in Journal of Environmental Science
news.summary: Our HPLC method development paper has been accepted, providing a validated approach for phenolic compound analysis in water matrices.
